Set up

library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)

nreturn_train <- read_csv("data/assignment_modeling.csv")

                              88                               88

                              88                               88

                              88                               88

                              88                               92

                              92                               88

                             122                               88

                              88                               88 
       pct_Civ_emp_16p_ACS_13_17      pct_Civ_unemp_16p_ACS_13_17 
                             116                              116 
     pct_Civ_emp_16_24_ACS_13_17    pct_Civ_unemp_16_24_ACS_13_17 
                             312                              312

                             180                              180

                             183                              183

                             111                               88

                              88                               88

                              88                               88

                             240                              119

                             313                             1413

                             132                              132

                             132                              132

    pct_HHD_PPL_Und_18_ACS_13_17     avg_Tot_Prns_in_HHD_CEN_2010 
                             132                                0 
   avg_Tot_Prns_in_HHD_ACS_13_17         pct_Rel_Under_6_CEN_2010 
                             132                               32 
       pct_Rel_Under_6_ACS_13_17       pct_HHD_Moved_in_ACS_13_17 
                             166                              132 
      pct_PUB_ASST_INC_ACS_13_17         avg_Agg_HH_INC_ACS_13_17 
                             132                              212 
     pct_Tot_Occp_Units_CEN_2010     pct_Tot_Occp_Units_ACS_13_17 
                               0                              121 
       pct_Vacant_Units_CEN_2010       pct_Vacant_Units_ACS_13_17 
                               0                              121 
     pct_Renter_Occp_HU_CEN_2010     pct_Renter_Occp_HU_ACS_13_17 
                               0                              132 
      pct_Owner_Occp_HU_CEN_2010      pct_Owner_Occp_HU_ACS_13_17 
                               0                              132 
       pct_Single_Unit_ACS_13_17      pct_MLT_U2_9_STRC_ACS_13_17 
                             121                              121 
          pct_MLT_U10p_ACS_13_17       pct_Mobile_Homes_ACS_13_17 
                             121                              121 
      pct_Crowd_Occp_U_ACS_13_17         pct_NO_PH_SRVC_ACS_13_17 
                             132                              132 
          pct_No_Plumb_ACS_13_17    pct_Recent_Built_HU_ACS_13_17 
                             819                              121 
   avg_Agg_House_Value_ACS_13_17    pct_MrdCple_w_child_ACS_13_17 
                            1431                              188 
   pct_HHD_NoCompDevic_ACS_13_17     pct_HHD_w_Computer_ACS_13_17 
                             132                              132 
   pct_HHD_w_OnlySPhne_ACS_13_17    pct_HHD_No_Internet_ACS_13_17 
                             132                              132 
   pct_HHD_w_Broadband_ACS_13_17 pct_TEA_MailOutMailBack_CEN_2010 
                             132                             3359 
   pct_TEA_Update_Leave_CEN_2010             pct_Vacants_CEN_2010 
                           50115                                0 
            pct_Deletes_CEN_2010          pct_Census_UAA_CEN_2010 
                               0                                0 
     pct_Mailback_Count_CEN_2010                  non_return_rate 
                               0                                0 
# Looks like there are a lot of variables that could cause data leakage, will need to drop. And the pct_TEA vars have a lot of missingness
data_leak_vars <- c("Low_Response_Score", "Self_Response_Rate_ACS_13_17", "pct_Deletes_CEN_2010", "pct_Census_UAA_CEN_2010", "pct_Mailback_Count_CEN_2010",
                    "pct_TEA_MailOutMailBack_CEN_2010", "pct_TEA_Update_Leave_CEN_2010", "pct_Vacants_CEN_2010")

# There are also a lot of duplicated variables - reporting the exact same thing, just as percentage vs count or from decennial census vs ACS
# Keeping percents and drop any variable for which there exists another variable that has the same name, plus the prefix "pct_"
# Keeping ACS data since it's more recent, and dropping decennial census duplicates
varnames <- names(nreturn_train)
pct_vars <- varnames[str_detect(varnames, "^pct_")]
nonpct_vars <- str_remove(pct_vars, "^pct_")
acs_vars <- varnames[str_detect(varnames, "ACS_13_17$")]
cen_dup_vars <- str_c(str_remove(acs_vars, "ACS_13_17$"), "CEN_2010")
dup_vars <- varnames[varnames %in% c(nonpct_vars, cen_dup_vars)]

vars_to_drop <- c(data_leak_vars, dup_vars)

# Get a sense of distribution of non-return rate
nreturn_train |>
  ggplot(aes(x = non_return_rate)) +
  geom_histogram()
# Check correlations between non-return rate and other vars
nreturn_train |>
  select(-all_of(vars_to_drop)) |>
  select(where(is.numeric)) |>
  correlate() |>
  focus(non_return_rate) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(non_return_rate))) |>
  head(30) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = term, y = non_return_rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 75)) +
  labs(x = "Variable", y = "Correlation with non-return rate")
# See if rates vary significantly across states
nreturn_train |>
  group_by(State_name) |>
  summarize(non_return_rate = mean(non_return_rate)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(State_name, non_return_rate), y = non_return_rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 75)) +
  labs(x = "State", y = "Average non-return rate")
# Some dollar variables are coded as characters, converting to numeric. Outside of recipe to save time during tuning/training
nreturn_train <- nreturn_train |>
  mutate(across(c(Aggregate_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, Aggr_House_Value_ACS_13_17,
      avg_Agg_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, avg_Agg_House_Value_ACS_13_17), 
      ~ as.numeric(gsub("[\\$,]", "", .))))

Set up folds

set.seed(20260420)
folds <- vfold_cv(data = nreturn_train, v = 3)

Tuning models

Lasso Regression

# parallelize
plan(multisession, workers = parallel::detectCores() - 2)

# recipe
lasso_rec <- recipe(formula = non_return_rate ~ ., data = nreturn_train) |>
  step_rm(!!vars_to_drop) |> # drop the duplicate/data leak variables
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |> # drop vars with zero variance
  add_role(GIDTR, County_name, new_role = "id") |> 
  step_rm(has_role("id")) |> # drop ID vars
  step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) |> # impute median for missing values
  step_log(Med_HHD_Inc_ACS_13_17, Aggregate_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, 
           Med_House_Value_ACS_13_17, Aggr_House_Value_ACS_13_17, 
           avg_Agg_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, avg_Agg_House_Value_ACS_13_17) |> # logging dollar value vars such as income, as they're likely very skewed
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) |>
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())

# model
lasso_mod <- linear_reg(penalty = tune(), mixture = 1) |>
  set_mode(mode = "regression") |>
  set_engine(engine = "glmnet")

# tune penalty
lasso_grid <- grid_regular(penalty(), levels = 20)

# workflow
lasso_wf <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(lasso_rec) |>
  add_model(lasso_mod)

# results
lasso_res <- lasso_wf |>
  tune_grid(resamples = folds,
            grid = lasso_grid,
            metrics = metric_set(yardstick::rmse, yardstick::mae))

lasso_res |>
  collect_metrics()
# A tibble: 40 × 7
    penalty .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err .config              
      <dbl> <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>                
 1 1   e-10 mae     standard    3.28     3 0.00524 Preprocessor1_Model01
 2 1   e-10 rmse    standard    4.84     3 0.0507  Preprocessor1_Model01
 3 3.36e-10 mae     standard    3.28     3 0.00524 Preprocessor1_Model02
 4 3.36e-10 rmse    standard    4.84     3 0.0507  Preprocessor1_Model02
 5 1.13e- 9 mae     standard    3.28     3 0.00524 Preprocessor1_Model03
 6 1.13e- 9 rmse    standard    4.84     3 0.0507  Preprocessor1_Model03
 7 3.79e- 9 mae     standard    3.28     3 0.00524 Preprocessor1_Model04
 8 3.79e- 9 rmse    standard    4.84     3 0.0507  Preprocessor1_Model04
 9 1.27e- 8 mae     standard    3.28     3 0.00524 Preprocessor1_Model05
10 1.27e- 8 rmse    standard    4.84     3 0.0507  Preprocessor1_Model05
# ℹ 30 more rows
lasso_res |>
  autoplot()
lasso_res |>
  show_best()
# A tibble: 5 × 7
   penalty .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err .config              
     <dbl> <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>                
1 1   e-10 rmse    standard    4.84     3  0.0507 Preprocessor1_Model01
2 3.36e-10 rmse    standard    4.84     3  0.0507 Preprocessor1_Model02
3 1.13e- 9 rmse    standard    4.84     3  0.0507 Preprocessor1_Model03
4 3.79e- 9 rmse    standard    4.84     3  0.0507 Preprocessor1_Model04
5 1.27e- 8 rmse    standard    4.84     3  0.0507 Preprocessor1_Model05

Regression Tree

# recipe
tree_rec <- recipe(formula = non_return_rate ~ ., data = nreturn_train) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |> # drop vars with zero variance
  add_role(GIDTR, County_name, new_role = "id") |> 
  step_rm(has_role("id")) |> # drop ID vars
  step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) |> # impute median for missing values
  step_log(Med_HHD_Inc_ACS_13_17, Aggregate_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, 
           Med_House_Value_ACS_13_17, Aggr_House_Value_ACS_13_17, 
           avg_Agg_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, avg_Agg_House_Value_ACS_13_17) |> # logging dollar value vars such as income, as they're likely very skewed
  step_rm(!!vars_to_drop) |> # drop the duplicate/data leak variables
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())

# model
tree_mod <- decision_tree(cost_complexity = tune(),
    tree_depth = tune(), min_n = tune()) |>
  set_engine(engine = "rpart") |>
  set_mode(mode = "regression")

# tune hyperparameters - cost complexity, tree depth, and minimum node size
tree_grid <- grid_max_entropy(
  cost_complexity(range = c(-5, -1)),
  tree_depth(),
  min_n(),
  size = 40
)

# workflow
tree_wf <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(tree_rec) |>
  add_model(tree_mod)

# results
tree_res <- tree_wf |>
  tune_grid(resamples = folds,
            grid = tree_grid,
            metrics = metric_set(yardstick::rmse, yardstick::mae))

tree_res |>
  collect_metrics()
# A tibble: 80 × 9
   cost_complexity tree_depth min_n .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err
             <dbl>      <int> <int> <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl>
 1       0.0166             8    32 mae     standard    4.37     3 0.0124 
 2       0.0166             8    32 rmse    standard    6.14     3 0.0473 
 3       0.000834           5    16 mae     standard    3.95     3 0.0105 
 4       0.000834           5    16 rmse    standard    5.65     3 0.00672
 5       0.0000204         12    22 mae     standard    3.75     3 0.00400
 6       0.0000204         12    22 rmse    standard    5.53     3 0.0412 
 7       0.000895          12    27 mae     standard    3.82     3 0.0120 
 8       0.000895          12    27 rmse    standard    5.49     3 0.0505 
 9       0.000531          14    27 mae     standard    3.76     3 0.0114 
10       0.000531          14    27 rmse    standard    5.45     3 0.0406 
# ℹ 70 more rows
# ℹ 1 more variable: .config <chr>
tree_res |>
  show_best()
# A tibble: 5 × 9
  cost_complexity tree_depth min_n .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err
            <dbl>      <int> <int> <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl>
1       0.0000367         10    34 rmse    standard    5.41     3  0.0417
2       0.0000830         12    36 rmse    standard    5.42     3  0.0451
3       0.000451          14    34 rmse    standard    5.42     3  0.0418
4       0.000296           8    36 rmse    standard    5.42     3  0.0407
5       0.000531          14    27 rmse    standard    5.45     3  0.0406
# ℹ 1 more variable: .config <chr>
tree_res |>
  autoplot()

Random Forest

# recipe
rf_rec <- recipe(formula = non_return_rate ~ ., data = nreturn_train) |>
  step_zv(all_predictors()) |> # drop vars with zero variance
  add_role(GIDTR, County_name, new_role = "id") |> 
  step_rm(has_role("id")) |> # drop ID vars
  step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) |> # impute median for missing values
  step_log(Med_HHD_Inc_ACS_13_17, Aggregate_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, 
           Med_House_Value_ACS_13_17, Aggr_House_Value_ACS_13_17, 
           avg_Agg_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, avg_Agg_House_Value_ACS_13_17) |> # logging dollar value vars such as income, as they're likely very skewed
  step_rm(!!vars_to_drop) |> # drop the duplicate/data leak variables
  step_corr(all_numeric_predictors(), threshold = 0.9) |> # get rid of highly correlated predictors to save computing time
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())

# model
rf_mod <- rand_forest(
  mtry = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  trees = 100
  ) |>
  set_mode(mode = "regression") |>
  set_engine(
    engine = "ranger", 
    importance = "impurity",
    num.threads = 4
  )

# tune hyperparameters - mtry and minimum node size
rf_grid <- grid_regular(
  mtry(range = c(5, 35)),
  min_n(range = c(5, 30)),
  levels = 5
)

# workflow
rf_wf <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rf_rec) |>
  add_model(rf_mod)


# results
set.seed(20260420)
rf_res <- rf_wf |>
  tune_grid(resamples = folds,
            grid = rf_grid,
            metrics = metric_set(yardstick::rmse, yardstick::mae))

rf_res |>
  collect_metrics()
# A tibble: 50 × 8
    mtry min_n .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err .config              
   <int> <int> <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>                
 1     5     5 mae     standard    3.41     3 0.00406 Preprocessor1_Model01
 2     5     5 rmse    standard    4.97     3 0.0348  Preprocessor1_Model01
 3    12     5 mae     standard    3.30     3 0.00490 Preprocessor1_Model02
 4    12     5 rmse    standard    4.84     3 0.0430  Preprocessor1_Model02
 5    20     5 mae     standard    3.26     3 0.00596 Preprocessor1_Model03
 6    20     5 rmse    standard    4.81     3 0.0433  Preprocessor1_Model03
 7    27     5 mae     standard    3.23     3 0.00198 Preprocessor1_Model04
 8    27     5 rmse    standard    4.79     3 0.0381  Preprocessor1_Model04
 9    35     5 mae     standard    3.22     3 0.00276 Preprocessor1_Model05
10    35     5 rmse    standard    4.76     3 0.0501  Preprocessor1_Model05
# ℹ 40 more rows
rf_res |>
  show_best()
# A tibble: 5 × 8
   mtry min_n .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err .config              
  <int> <int> <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>                
1    35    23 rmse    standard    4.76     3  0.0410 Preprocessor1_Model20
2    35     5 rmse    standard    4.76     3  0.0501 Preprocessor1_Model05
3    35    11 rmse    standard    4.77     3  0.0449 Preprocessor1_Model10
4    27    17 rmse    standard    4.77     3  0.0446 Preprocessor1_Model14
5    35    17 rmse    standard    4.77     3  0.0364 Preprocessor1_Model15
rf_res |>
  autoplot()

Random forest had better performance than Lasso and a single regression tree; I’ll now use this model for the final fit on the entire training dataset. For tuning, I used 100 trees to save time, but I will use 500 for training the final model.

Training final model on full training set

# select best parameters from random forest results
best_rf <- rf_res |>
  select_best(metric = "rmse")

# create best model from random forest results, updating trees from 100 to 500
final_mod <- rand_forest(
  mtry = best_rf$mtry,
  min_n = best_rf$min_n,
  trees = 500
  ) |>
  set_mode(mode = "regression") |>
  set_engine(
    engine = "ranger", 
    importance = "impurity",
    num.threads = 4
  )

# final workflow
final_wf <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rf_rec) |>
  add_model(final_mod)

# fit the final model on entire training set
set.seed(20260420)
final_fit <- final_wf |>
  fit(data = nreturn_train)

Generate final predictions

nreturn_test <- read_csv("data/assignment_implementation.csv") |>
  mutate(across(c(Aggregate_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, Aggr_House_Value_ACS_13_17,
      avg_Agg_HH_INC_ACS_13_17, avg_Agg_House_Value_ACS_13_17), 
      ~ as.numeric(gsub("[\\$,]", "", .)))) # fixing dollar variables are coded as characters

predictions <- bind_cols(
  nreturn_test,
  predict(final_fit, new_data = nreturn_test)
) |>
  select(GIDTR, .pred)

write_csv(predictions, "data/predictions.csv")
